title: messages: FileHeader.size_bytes uncapped; receiver may pre-allocate u64::MAX bytes
description: FileHeader.size_bytes: u64 has no enforced upper bound at deserialization. A hostile peer can send size_bytes: u64::MAX with total_chunks: 1. The CLI receiver checks size_bytes > 10 MiB and drops the transfer (cli/src/commands.rs:1225), but (1) the message is fully deserialized including allocation of the FileHeader struct before that check runs — not a problem for the struct itself, but (2) a receiver that pre-allocates a Vec::with_capacity(size_bytes) based on this field before the application-level check would OOM. The safe fix is to reject size_bytes > max_chunk_size * total_chunks at the protocol boundary, or at minimum cap size_bytes to match the total_chunks*10MiB product.
